title: # DNN-HA Real-time Hearing-Aid Web GUI
desc: This is a hearing-aid processing simulator based on a deep neural network. You can upload audio or use a microphone to experience the difference between the 'Original sound heard by hearing-impaired' and the 'AI hearing-aid compensated sound'.
in_SNR_lbl: Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR, leave empty for no noise)
mic_tip:
> [!TIP]
> It is recommended to wear headphones while using Real-time Mode to avoid feedback.
mic_warn: Note: Real-time mode may have slight latency depending on CPU and browser performance.
ana_desc: View the time-domain waveform, spectrogram, and 1/3 octave band analysis of the audio. Please process audio in previous tabs or click the 'Analyze' button first.
audio_title:
### Configure Hearing Loss by Frequency
(dB HL)
tone_desc: Adjust frequency and volume as a reference for hearing tests or equipment calibration. This uses the browser's Web Audio API for real-time, zero-latency output.
err_no_audio: Error: Please upload or select an audio file first!
err_frame_size_small: Error: Frame Size cannot be less than 64!
err_frame_size_mul: Error: Frame Size must be a multiple of 64 (e.g. 256, 512, 1024)!
msg_proc_done:
Processing complete! Time taken: {time:.4f} sec.
Model predicted output SPL: {spl:.2f} dB SPL
💡(Note: Simulated audio reflects true attenuation; AI Compensated audio has been amplified to optimal normal hearing levels (representing HA gain).)
msg_audio_saved: ✅ Audiogram successfully saved! Will be loaded automatically on next startup.
msg_audio_fmt_err: ❌ Format error: Expected a JSON list containing 9 numbers
